summary: Live-verified endpoint that returns paginated federal account funding rows for a single award, with reporting-period, agency, object class, and program activity context.
businessPurpose: Use this endpoint to explain how one award is funded across federal accounts and reporting periods, including obligated and outlay amounts and the agencies and accounting classifications attached to each funding row.
analyticalGrain: One row per award funding/accounting slice for a specific reporting fiscal period and agency/account/program-object-class combination within a single requested award.

primaryEntities.name: Award
primaryEntities.description: The endpoint is scoped to exactly one requested award_id per call.

primaryEntities.name: Federal account funding row
primaryEntities.description: Each result row represents a funding-related accounting slice attached to the award, identified by federal account, agencies, object class, program activity, and reporting period fields.

measures.name: Transaction obligated amount
measures.description: Obligated amount reported for the funding row.

measures.name: Gross outlay amount
measures.description: Gross outlay amount reported for the funding row; live data can include null values.

dimensions.name: Reporting fiscal period
dimensions.description: Rows are labeled by reporting fiscal year, quarter, and month, plus whether the submission is quarterly.

dimensions.name: Federal account
dimensions.description: Rows include federal_account and account_title to identify the account receiving or providing funding context.

dimensions.name: Funding and awarding agencies
dimensions.description: Rows include both funding and awarding agency ids, names, top-tier ids, and slugs.

dimensions.name: Program activity and object class
dimensions.description: Rows include program activity code/name and object class code/name so callers can explain how funding is classified within the award.

dimensions.name: Disaster/emergency fund code
dimensions.description: Rows include disaster_emergency_fund_code, but its type and meaning need careful handling because docs type it as boolean while live data returned code values or null.

suitableQuestions.name: Which federal accounts and agencies are associated with a given award's funding rows?
suitableQuestions.description: The endpoint is well suited for inspecting federal_account, account_title, and the awarding/funding agency context for one award.

suitableQuestions.name: How do obligated and outlay amounts vary across reporting periods for one award?
suitableQuestions.description: Use reporting_fiscal_year/quarter/month together with transaction_obligated_amount and gross_outlay_amount to study time-sliced funding activity for one award.

suitableQuestions.name: Which object classes or program activities appear in the funding history of one award?
suitableQuestions.description: The endpoint returns object class and program activity fields on each row, enabling classification-oriented inspection for a single award.

notSuitableFor.name: Searching across many awards
notSuitableFor.description: This endpoint requires one award_id and does not provide a multi-award search surface.

notSuitableFor.name: Computing a complete count of funding rows before paging
notSuitableFor.description: Neither the docs nor the current raw profile indicate a total-count field in page_metadata.

notSuitableFor.name: Treating results as a simple award total without grain checks
notSuitableFor.description: Rows are accounting slices over time and classification dimensions, so naive aggregation can misrepresent the award if the caller assumes each row is a unique total snapshot.

joins.description: Use award-level endpoints to retrieve broader award metadata, then use this endpoint to inspect funding composition for the same award identifier.

joins.to: Agency reference data or agency-focused USAspending endpoints
joins.description: Agency identifiers and slugs in each row can be aligned with agency reference surfaces when the caller needs hierarchy or display metadata beyond this endpoint.

workflows.name: Inspect funding composition for one known award
workflows.description: Given a known award identifier, page through funding rows to explain account, agency, and reporting-period context for that award.

steps.action: Provide a generated award_id when possible, since the docs prefer the natural generated identifier over deprecated numeric surrogate ids.

Start from the safe template request shape {award_id, page, limit, sort, order} and call POST /api/v2/awards/funding/; a reviewer-confirmed safe-template request with page=1 and order=desc returned HTTP 200.

Interpret each row as a funding/accounting slice and continue paging with page_metadata.next or hasNext until the needed coverage is collected; the reviewer-confirmed safe-template call echoed page_metadata.page=1.

Set sort and order explicitly if row order matters, because the documented default sort conflicts with the observed default behavior; page=1 and order=desc were both exercised successfully in a reviewer-confirmed safe-template call.

caveats.statement: The documented default sort conflicts with live behavior, so callers should always set sort and order explicitly when row ordering matters; a reviewer-confirmed safe-template call succeeded with page=1, sort=reporting_fiscal_date, and order=desc.
caveats.impact: Avoid relying on implied defaults for deterministic pagination or comparisons.

caveats.statement: Unknown award ids may return HTTP 200 with an empty results array according to the current raw profile, even though this bundle did not re-probe that case.
caveats.impact: Empty results should not be treated as proof that the request was semantically valid without validating the award id elsewhere.

caveats.statement: The meaning and type of disaster_emergency_fund_code are not cleanly documented; live data returned string codes or null, not booleans.
caveats.impact: Preserve the raw field and avoid reducing it to a boolean or enumerated business label without outside corroboration.

caveats.statement: Gross outlay amounts may be null on otherwise valid rows.
caveats.impact: Aggregation and display logic should handle null outlays explicitly.
